description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a yellow upper and lower wing, featuring black heraldic lion emblems on the wings. The aircraft has a dual-engine configuration mounted between the wings, with exposed engine components and propellers at the front. The fuselage is slender and painted yellow, with a forward open cockpit situated just behind the engines. The tail assembly is distinctive, comprising three vertical stabilizers and two horizontal stabilizers, all supported by a red framework. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The structure is reinforced with visible struts and wire bracing, typical of early 20th-century aircraft.
description: A yellow vintage biplane with dual exposed engines, open cockpit, and a triple-fin tail, featuring black lion emblems on its wings.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the yellow wings with black lion emblems, triple-fin tail, and open cockpit behind the engines.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual exposed engines, propellers, and the full span of the biplane's wings with visible struts and bracing.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the red undercarriage, fixed landing gear, and the structure of the tail assembly.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the triple-fin tail, horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the wings and fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane with straight, high-mounted wings, no winglets, and prominent interplane struts and wire bracing.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted between the wings with exposed cylinders and front-facing propellers.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with three vertical stabilizers and two horizontal stabilizers supported by a red framework.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engines, likely single or tandem seat with no enclosure.
